program.name: Supplemental Security Income (SSI)
program.description_short: Federal cash assistance for individuals who are disabled, blind, or 65 years of age or older
program.description:
Supplemental Security Income (SSI) is a federal needs-based program that provides monthly cash to help meet your food, shelter, and personal care needs.

If approved, the Social Security Administration deposits your benefit directly to a bank account or onto a Direct Express debit card each month. Washington does not pay a state supplement for most people, so your benefit will be the federal amount.

Receiving SSI may affect the value of other cash assistance benefits, including TANF. An additional resource limit (typically $2,000 for individuals or $3,000 for couples) applies and is verified at the application stage.

It may take 6 to 12 months to receive an initial decision. Many claims are denied at first and approved on appeal — apply online at ssa.gov, by phone at 1-800-772-1213, or in person at your local SSA office.
program.website_description: Federal cash assistance for individuals who are disabled, blind, or 65 years of age or older

warning_messages.message: If you get SSDI, the amount of your SSI payment may be lower. In some cases, you may not get SSI if you also get SSDI.
It may take 6 to 8 months to get an initial decision. Your claim may be denied at first. Claims are often approved on appeal, but approval can sometimes take up to 3 years.

documents.text: Proof of home address (ex: lease, mortgage statement, utility bill)
Proof of unearned income (ex: child support, social security, unemployment)
Proof of earned income (ex: pay stubs, tax returns, W-2, 1099) & employer information
Proof of identity (ex: driver's license, official state ID card)
Proof of status in U.S. (ex: birth certificate, green card, work permit)

navigators.name: Northwest Justice Project - CLEAR Hotline
navigators.description: Statewide legal aid hotline for low-income Washington residents. CLEAR provides legal advice and referrals on SSI applications, denials, and appeals. Outside King County: 1-888-201-1014 (weekdays 9:15am-12:15pm). King County residents: dial 2-1-1. Adults age 60+: 1-888-387-7111 (CLEAR*Sr).

navigators.name: Benefits Law Center
navigators.description: Free legal advocacy for people with disabilities who are homeless or low-income and have had their Supplemental Security Income (SSI) or Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) reduced, denied, or terminated. Primary intake serves King County and the greater Puget Sound area; targeted projects extend assistance to Western Washington through partner agencies. Office: 1404 E Yesler Way, Suite 203, Seattle, WA 98122.
